column1: How many points does the race winner receive in the current Formula 1 scoring system?
In what year and at which circuit was the first Formula 1 Grand Prix held?
How many Formula 1 World Drivers Championships did Michael Schumacher win in total?
What does a yellow flag with red stripes indicate in Formula 1?
column2: The existence on the track of an element that causes a reduction in grip, such as oil residues or car fragments

column1: Why did the FIA ban electronic driver aids such as traction control and semi-automatic gearboxes for the 1994 season?
column2: Because of complaints that technology was determining race results more than driver skill

column1: How many points did first place receive in the 1950-1959 scoring system compared to the current system?
column2: 8 points in 1950-1959 compared to 25 points in the current system

column1: Which company has been responsible for managing and operating the Formula 1 championship since September 2016?
What was the greatest technological advance introduced by Lotus in 1962?
column2: An aluminium monocoque chassis instead of the traditional tubular frame

column1: What event led to the implementation of the virtual safety car system in Formula 1?
column2: The death of Jules Bianchi at the 2014 Japanese Grand Prix after crashing into a recovery crane at turn 7

column1: How many dry-weather tyre compounds are available in Formula 1 from 2024, and what are they called?
column2: Five compounds from C1 to C5, named hard (white), medium (yellow) and soft (red) for race use
